title: ISO 25010 Quality Model Overview
content: ISO/IEC 25010 defines 8 product quality characteristics: Functional Suitability; Performance Efficiency; Compatibility; Usability; Reliability; Security; Maintainability; Portability. Each characteristic contains sub-characteristics used to specify and measure quality attributes.
tags: ISO25010 quality-model characteristics NFR framework

title: ISO 25010 Functional Suitability
content: Functional Suitability covers 3 sub-characteristics: Completeness (all tasks covered); Correctness (results accurate); Appropriateness (tasks facilitate objectives). Core capability of the system.
tags: functional-suitability completeness correctness appropriateness ISO25010

title: ISO 25010 Performance Efficiency
content: Performance Efficiency covers: Time Behavior (response time latency); Resource Utilization (CPU memory disk); Capacity (throughput concurrent users). Measured at P95/P99 percentiles under load.
tags: performance-efficiency time-behavior resource-utilization capacity ISO25010

title: ISO 25010 Compatibility
content: Compatibility covers: Co-existence (sharing resources without impact); Interoperability (exchange and use information with other systems). Critical for integration-heavy systems.
tags: compatibility co-existence interoperability ISO25010

title: ISO 25010 Usability
content: Usability covers: Learnability; Operability; User Error Protection; UI Aesthetics; Accessibility. New users should complete basic task within 10 minutes; core tasks in 3 clicks.
tags: usability learnability operability accessibility ISO25010 UX

title: ISO 25010 Reliability
content: Reliability covers: Maturity; Availability; Fault Tolerance; Recoverability. Systems must maintain uptime targets and recover from failures within defined RTO/RPO.
tags: reliability availability fault-tolerance recoverability ISO25010 RTO RPO

title: ISO 25010 Security
content: Security covers: Confidentiality; Integrity; Non-repudiation; Accountability; Authenticity. Maps to CIA Triad plus AAA (Authentication Authorization Accounting).
tags: security confidentiality integrity non-repudiation accountability authenticity CIA-triad ISO25010

title: ISO 25010 Maintainability
content: Maintainability covers: Modularity; Reusability; Analysability; Modifiability; Testability. Code coverage >= 80% unit tests; rollback within 5 minutes.
tags: maintainability modularity reusability testability modifiability ISO25010

title: ISO 25010 Portability
content: Portability covers: Adaptability; Installability; Replaceability. System should support deployment across environments without architecture changes.
tags: portability adaptability installability replaceability ISO25010

title: NFR Specification Template
content: Standard NFR template: NFR-[CATEGORY]-[NUMBER]; Title; ISO 25010 mapping; Priority (Critical/High/Medium/Low); Requirement statement using SHALL; Measurement (metric method tool frequency); Targets table (normal/peak); Rationale; Verification.
tags: NFR-template specification SHALL measurement verification priority

title: NFR Writing Pattern - SHALL Statement
content: NFR writing pattern: "The system SHALL [action/behavior] [measurement] under [conditions]." Example: The system SHALL respond to API requests within 200ms measured at P95 under normal load of 500 concurrent users.
tags: SHALL-pattern NFR-writing measurable conditions performance

title: Response Time Benchmarks by Operation Type
content: Response time targets by operation: Page load <2s target/<3s max; API response <200ms target/<500ms max; Search query <500ms target/<1s max; Report generation <5s target/<10s max; File upload 10MB <3s target. All measured at P95.
tags: response-time latency benchmarks API page-load search performance

title: Throughput and Capacity Requirements
content: Capacity targets: 10000 concurrent users; 500 TPS normal/1500 TPS peak for 30min; 30000 RPM at API gateway; 100TB total storage; 10 million daily transactions; 500000 monthly active users. Scale 2x without architecture change; 5x with horizontal scaling.
tags: throughput capacity TPS concurrent-users RPM scaling performance

title: Resource Utilization Limits
content: Resource utilization targets: CPU <50% normal/<80% max (alert at 70%); Memory <60% normal/<85% max (alert at 75%); Disk <70% normal/<90% max (alert at 80%); Network bandwidth <50%/<80%; Database connections <70%/<90%; Thread pool <60%/<85%.
tags: resource-utilization CPU memory disk network performance

title: Availability SLA Tiers
content: Availability tiers: Standard 99.0% (87.6hr/yr downtime) for internal tools; Business 99.5% (43.8hr); Premium 99.9% (8.76hr) for customer-facing; Critical 99.95% (4.38hr) for e-commerce; Mission 99.99% (52.6min) for healthcare/banking; Ultra 99.999% (5.26min) for life safety.
tags: availability SLA uptime downtime tiers 99.9 99.99

title: Availability Calculation Formula
content: Availability % = (Total Time - Downtime) / Total Time × 100%. Measured monthly. Excludes scheduled maintenance communicated 48hrs ahead. MTTR and MTBF used as supporting metrics.
tags: availability-formula calculation downtime MTTR MTBF SLA

title: Disaster Recovery Objectives
content: DR objectives: RTO (Recovery Time Objective) 4 hours max time to restore service; RPO (Recovery Point Objective) 1 hour max acceptable data loss; MTTR 2 hours average recovery time. DR test quarterly. Strategies: Hot Standby RTO<1min; Warm Standby 1-4hr; Cold Standby 4-24hr.
tags: disaster-recovery RTO RPO MTTR hot-standby warm-standby DR backup

title: Fault Tolerance Patterns
content: Fault tolerance patterns required: Circuit Breaker for external dependencies; Retry with exponential backoff; Bulkhead isolation for critical services; Health checks and auto-healing. Automatic failover for DB primary failure <30s; graceful degradation on network partition.
tags: fault-tolerance circuit-breaker bulkhead retry failover patterns reliability

title: OWASP Top 10 Mitigation Checklist
content: OWASP mitigations: Broken Access Control→RBAC least privilege; Cryptographic Failures→TLS1.3 AES-256; Injection→parameterized queries; Insecure Design→threat modeling; Security Misconfiguration→hardening; Vulnerable Components→dependency scanning; Auth Failures→MFA session mgmt; Integrity Failures→signed artifacts; Logging Failures→SIEM; SSRF→allowlist.
tags: OWASP security vulnerabilities mitigation checklist top-10

title: Authentication Requirements Pattern
content: Authentication spec: Username+Password primary; MFA required for privileged access (TOTP app or Hardware key); SSO via SAML2.0/OAuth2.0/OIDC; JWT access token 15min expiry; refresh token 7 days rotated on use. Password: 12 chars min; complexity required; 5 failed attempts lockout 30min.
tags: authentication MFA SSO JWT password-policy OWASP security

title: Data Classification Levels
content: Data classification: PUBLIC freely available (marketing); INTERNAL restricted to org (policies); CONFIDENTIAL restricted access (financial data PII); RESTRICTED highly sensitive (passwords keys PHI). Encryption: at rest AES-256; in transit TLS1.3; key management via HSM or Cloud KMS; annual key rotation.
tags: data-classification encryption AES-256 TLS PII PHI confidentiality security

title: GDPR/PII Compliance Requirements
content: PII handling requirements: Data minimization collect only what needed; Anonymization/Pseudonymization where possible; Retention policy delete when no longer needed; Right to be forgotten support data deletion requests; Breach notification within 72 hours; Data processing records maintained.
tags: GDPR PII privacy data-protection compliance retention right-to-be-forgotten

title: WCAG 2.1 Level AA Accessibility Checklist
content: WCAG 2.1 AA requirements: Screen reader compatible; Keyboard-only navigation for all functions; Color contrast ratio >=4.5:1; Text resizable to 200%; No content flashing >3 times/second; Alt text for images; Captions for video; Focus indicators visible.
tags: WCAG accessibility screen-reader keyboard color-contrast usability

title: Usability Targets
content: Usability metrics: New user completes basic task within 10 minutes without training; core tasks completable in <=3 clicks from dashboard; confirmation required for destructive actions; auto-save drafts every 60 seconds; progress indicators for operations >2 seconds.
tags: usability learnability operability user-error-protection metrics targets

title: Maintainability NFR Pattern
content: Maintainability requirements: Microservices with bounded contexts; loose coupling; API versioning; code coverage >=80% unit tests; integration tests for all APIs; E2E tests for critical journeys; externalized configuration; scripted/versioned DB migrations; blue-green or canary deployment; structured JSON logging; OpenTelemetry tracing.
tags: maintainability testability modifiability microservices CI-CD logging tracing

title: Cloud-Specific NFR Pattern
content: Cloud NFRs: Horizontal auto-scaling CPU>70% for 5min scale-out / CPU<30% for 15min scale-in; max 10x baseline; new instances <5min; Multi-AZ minimum 2 zones; cross-region DR; tenant data isolation separate DB or schemas; resource quotas per tenant; cost anomaly alerts >20% variance.
tags: cloud scalability elasticity multi-AZ multi-tenancy auto-scaling cost-optimization

title: SLA Template Structure
content: SLA template sections: 1.Service Level Objectives (availability/response time/error rate/incident response); 2.Incident Severity Definitions (P1-P4 response/resolution times); 3.Service Credits (10-50% for missed targets); 4.Exclusions (scheduled maintenance customer-caused force majeure).
tags: SLA template SLO incident-severity service-credits exclusions

title: Incident Severity Definitions
content: Incident severity: P1 complete outage all users - response 15min/resolution 4hrs; P2 major functionality impaired - 30min/8hrs; P3 minor functionality impaired - 4hrs/24hrs; P4 cosmetic low impact - 1 business day/5 business days.
tags: incident-severity P1 P2 P3 P4 response-time resolution SLA

title: Service Credit Formula
content: SLA service credits: 99.9%-99.5% uptime = 10% credit; 99.5%-99.0% = 25% credit; <99.0% = 50% credit. Credits applied to next billing period. Not applicable if downtime caused by customer or force majeure.
tags: SLA service-credits uptime billing formula

title: NFR Quality Checklist
content: NFR quality checklist: Unique ID per NFR; ISO 25010 mapping; measurable quantified target; defined measurement method; verification approach; no ambiguous terms (fast/easy/user-friendly without definition). Coverage: performance/reliability/security/usability/maintainability/compliance all addressed. Feasibility reviewed by technical team.
tags: NFR-quality checklist measurable verification ISO25010 coverage feasibility

title: Planguage Specification Template
content: Planguage NFR specification attributes: Tag (unique identifier); Ambition (goal); Scale (measurement scale); Meter (how measured); Must (minimum acceptable); Plan (target to achieve); Wish (ideal target); Past (historical baseline); Trend (direction of change). Eliminates ambiguity in NFR statements.
tags: planguage NFR-specification attributes meter scale must plan wish ambiguity

title: Performance NFR by Domain Benchmarks
content: Domain-specific performance benchmarks: E-commerce checkout <2s; Banking transactions <3s; Healthcare record retrieval <1s; Real-time trading <100ms; Content streaming <500ms buffer start. These are industry-accepted standards for customer-facing systems.
tags: performance-benchmarks domain e-commerce banking healthcare trading streaming

title: NFR ID Naming Convention
content: NFR naming convention: NFR-[CATEGORY]-[NUMBER] where CATEGORY = PERF (performance); REL (reliability); SEC (security); USE (usability); MAIN (maintainability); COMP (compatibility); PORT (portability); CLOUD (cloud). Example: NFR-PERF-001 NFR-SEC-003.
tags: NFR-naming convention ID category PERF REL SEC USE MAIN

title: Security CIA Triad Framework
content: Security framework: CIA Triad (Confidentiality Privacy; Integrity Correctness; Availability Access) plus AAA (Authentication who are you; Authorization what can you do; Accounting audit trail). All security NFRs map to one or more of these pillars.
tags: CIA-triad authentication authorization accounting security framework

title: NFR Coverage Coverage Checklist
content: NFR coverage: Performance defined; Reliability/Availability defined; Security defined (authentication data protection OWASP compliance); Usability defined (WCAG if applicable); Maintainability defined (testability modifiability); Compliance identified (GDPR HIPAA PCI etc). All 8 ISO 25010 characteristics considered.
tags: NFR-coverage checklist ISO25010 performance security usability compliance

title: P95 P99 Percentile Measurement
content: Percentile measurement: P95 means 95% of requests complete within target time; P99 used for background/batch processing; P50 (median) for average case; real-time operations measured at P95; mission-critical at P99. Tools: APM systems like New Relic Datadog Dynatrace.
tags: percentile P95 P99 latency measurement APM monitoring performance

title: NFR Trade-off Patterns
content: Common NFR trade-offs: Performance vs Security (encryption adds latency); Availability vs Consistency (CAP theorem); Usability vs Security (MFA friction); Maintainability vs Performance (abstraction overhead). Document trade-off decisions with rationale.
tags: NFR-tradeoffs performance security availability consistency usability maintainability

title: Scalability vs Elasticity
content: Scalability = ability to handle growing load by adding resources (scale-up or scale-out). Elasticity = ability to dynamically provision/deprovision resources based on demand. Cloud systems need both: scale out when CPU>70% for 5min; scale in when CPU<30% for 15min; max 10x baseline capacity.
tags: scalability elasticity cloud auto-scaling horizontal vertical capacity

title: NFR Verification Approach Types
content: NFR verification methods: Inspection (review specification); Demonstration (show behavior); Test (load test penetration test); Analysis (modeling simulation). Load testing tools: JMeter Gatling k6. Security: annual third-party penetration test. Monitoring: continuous APM alerting in production.
tags: NFR-verification inspection demonstration load-test penetration-test APM monitoring

title: Multi-Tenancy Isolation Requirements
content: Multi-tenancy requirements: Tenant data isolation via separate databases or schemas; resource quotas per tenant to prevent noisy neighbor; no tenant data leakage verified by audit; per-tenant logging and audit trails; tenant-specific SLAs may apply; data residency requirements honored.
tags: multi-tenancy isolation tenant data-security quotas audit SaaS cloud
